reason: Backend spec entry point with Pre-Development Checklist and Quality Check; required reading before any backend change.

Defines the deterministic SetupContextPacket -> governed history -> runtime overlay -> final request messages contract. SkillPack must inject into this assembly without breaking ordering or final message shape.
wizard_stage is a new turn-input field that must thread through SetupAgentExecutionService preflight/launch boundary without splitting text/stream paths or thickening the harness.

Existing stage-driven runtime narrowing precedent. Pilot reuses the same stage-signal-from-turn-input pattern for SkillPack selection but does not change tool_scope behavior — read to keep the two stage signals consistent.
Stage-local context governance contract. SkillPack is stage-local content; its rendering must not violate digest/tool-outcome retention or no-progress guards.
Compact prior-stage handoff contract. SkillPack must not duplicate or shadow prior_stage_handoffs — these are orthogonal stage signals, and Pilot must keep that boundary clean.
Runtime-v2 only entrypoint convergence. Confirms which factory/entrypoint owns turn input wiring; wizard_stage must flow through the converged path, not a transitional one.
SetupGraph shell stays focused on checkpoint/routing only. SkillPack injection MUST happen in SetupAgentPromptService, not in the graph shell — read to avoid that mistake.
Turn-loop order, continue/finish taxonomy. SkillPack rendering must not perturb continue_reason / finish_reason semantics or runtime trace shape.

Pydantic validation and one-shot schema repair contract. SkillPack content_skeleton is recommendation-only; must NOT introduce strict schema validation paths that would conflict with this repair flow.
Frontend spec entry point. Pilot adds wizardStage transmission from prestory_setup_page.dart turn dispatch — must follow checklist and quality bar.
Frontend state-management conventions. _selectedStage already exists as state; the change reads it and adds it to the outgoing turn payload, must align with this guideline.
Type safety guidance for the Dart turn-request model when adding the wizard_stage field.
Cross-layer thinking. Pilot is a clean cross-layer change (frontend signal -> backend prompt path); read before designing the contract surface.

Code reuse guidance. Stage-aware tool scope (already in repo) is the existing precedent for stage-driven runtime behavior; the SkillPack module must reuse the same stage-signal extraction pattern rather than re-inventing it.
